questions.stem: A patient presents with the constellation of findings depicted here (blind as a bat, dry as a bone, red as a beet, hot as a hare, mad as a hatter). Which agent is used to reverse this toxidrome?

explanation.summary: The illustration depicts the anticholinergic (antimuscarinic) toxidrome. Physostigmine, a centrally acting acetylcholinesterase inhibitor, raises synaptic acetylcholine to overcome muscarinic blockade and is the antidote. Atropine would worsen it, pralidoxime treats organophosphate poisoning, naloxone reverses opioids, and flumazenil reverses benzodiazepines.
explanation.whyCorrect: The illustration depicts the anticholinergic (antimuscarinic) toxidrome. Physostigmine, a centrally acting acetylcholinesterase inhibitor, raises synaptic acetylcholine to overcome muscarinic blockade and is the antidote. Atropine would worsen it, pralidoxime treats organophosphate poisoning, naloxone reverses opioids, and flumazenil reverses benzodiazepines.

questions.stem: Based on this illustration of a cholinergic synapse, how do the antagonist molecules shown at the target cell membrane produce their pharmacologic effect?

choices.label: A
choices.text: They block the release of acetylcholine from the synaptic vesicles

choices.label: B
choices.text: They competitively occupy the muscarinic receptors, preventing acetylcholine from binding

choices.label: C
choices.text: They directly activate the receptors to mimic acetylcholine

choices.label: D
choices.text: They enzymatically degrade acetylcholine within the synaptic cleft

choices.label: E
choices.text: They prevent the nerve impulse from reaching the presynaptic terminal

explanation.summary: Anticholinergics are muscarinic antagonists that act by competitive inhibition, binding the receptor without activating it so that endogenous acetylcholine cannot exert its effect on the target cell.
explanation.whyCorrect: Anticholinergics are muscarinic antagonists that act by competitive inhibition, binding the receptor without activating it so that endogenous acetylcholine cannot exert its effect on the target cell.
